data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN (updated 2023); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; CGMFPFED Portal

Q15.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 ('185,000 registered confirmed — enrollment database'); Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q16.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26 — all 185,000 collectors are tribal households residing in forest-fringe villages, physically present within the operational forest commons area

Q17.v: Settled_communities — tribal villages within the forest operational zones of Chhattisgarh (within GP/block boundary of the forest collection area)
Q17.r: NTFP collectors are sedentary tribal households resident in forest-fringe villages. Their collection activity requires physical proximity to the forest commons. All registered collectors must be present within the GP/block jurisdiction of the operational collection centre (1,250 centres covering state forest divisions). 'Local' is defined as within the GP/block of the forest commons resource per the settled-community definition.
Q17.src: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 (185,000 tribal households described as forest-fringe dependent)

Q18.interp: PASS — θ₂ = 1.00, well above 0.70 threshold. All registered scheme beneficiaries are local tribal households residing within the operational forest commons area.
Q18.src: Derived from Q15 and Q16; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q19.r: All 185,000 registered collectors are tribal (Scheduled Tribe) households — the primary marginalised category under the expanded definition (SC/ST/de-notified tribes/landless). Additionally, tribal women constitute 65% of collectors (116,500 HH women-led or women-primary collectors), further reinforcing marginalised beneficiary classification. No non-tribal, non-marginalised households are included as collectors in this scheme.
Q19.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26 ('tribal women 65%'); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (185,000 tribal registered confirmed); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q20.interp: PASS — θ₃ = 1.00, well above 0.40 threshold. 100% of beneficiaries are tribal (ST) households, all qualifying under the expanded marginalised definition.
Q20.src: Derived from Q15 and Q19; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

22a.r: 35 tribal producer cooperatives formally registered under CGMFPFED (Chhattisgarh Minor Forest Produce Federation) framework. Cooperative membership: 145,000 collectors (out of 185,000 total). CGMFPFED itself functions as the apex institutional body with state government mandate. Type: Tribal producer cooperative groups with CGMFPFED as the institutional umbrella.

22b.r: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN mandates cooperative structure for collective marketing as a core scheme component (item 5: 'Cooperatives: 35 producer groups (collective marketing)'). The ₹40 cr cooperative support budget line confirms this is a mandated institutional requirement, not optional. Cooperative participation in price negotiation and collective offtake is integral to scheme design.

22c.r: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 independently verified '35 registered confirmed (CGMFPFED records)' with operational status. CGMFPFED portal lists all 35 cooperatives as active. The cooperatives executed collective bargaining during FY 2024-25 resulting in documented price outcomes above MPP, which requires functional governance and decision-making meetings. CAG verified transaction records confirming active cooperative operations. Note: Explicit meeting minutes count (≥2 meetings with resolutions) is not separately cited in the reviewed sources; functionality is inferred from CAG-verified operational outputs and CGMFPFED registration compliance requirements.

docs: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (cooperative verification section)
CGMFPFED Portal — cooperative directory (35 active listings)
Q22.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; CGMFPFED Portal; Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN

Q23.r: Tribal women constitute 65% of active collectors (Outcome Budget 2025-26). Given that cooperatives represent the collector base and women form 65% of that base, it is reasonable to infer women constitute ≥33% of cooperative governance positions. Additionally, cooperatives in Fifth Schedule (PESA) areas of Chhattisgarh are subject to Gram Panchayat provisions implementing the 73rd Constitutional Amendment's 33% women reservation requirement. Note: Explicit documentation of ≥33% women in cooperative executive/office-bearing positions is not separately cited; inference is based on 65% women collector share and PESA governance requirements.
Q23.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26 ('tribal women 65%'); PESA 1996 (33% women reservation in local governance); 73rd Constitutional Amendment

Q24.r: No formal grievance or voice mechanism specifically mandated at the scheme level for non-beneficiaries (tribal collectors excluded from registration or price support) is mentioned in any reviewed source. The CGMFPFED portal provides information and registration services but no explicit complaint/grievance portal for excluded individuals. No Social Audit provision or Ombudsman specific to this scheme is referenced in the Official Notification or Outcome Budget. This constitutes a governance gap — flagged accordingly.
Q24.src: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN (no grievance mechanism mentioned); CGMFPFED Portal (no grievance portal referenced); Outcome Budget 2025-26

pass_verification.enh_T2.4_flag: GOVERNANCE GAP — T2.4 = 0: No formal grievance mechanism mandated at scheme level for excluded beneficiaries. Combined with any other absent enhancement variable, this triggers 'SOC with Governance Gaps' classification.
pass_verification.enh_T2.5_flag: ok — Women ≥33% representation inferred from 65% women collector base and PESA requirements.
